zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Maddox於1732年1月1日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州古倫德時，她的父親John Maddox Sr32歲，母親Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood30歲。她於1755年10月15日與John D. Hancock在美國維吉尼亞州古倫德聖 加也斯 帕里什結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1805年在美國伊利諾州埃德加芭黎絲享年73歲，埋葬在美國伊利諾州埃德加芭黎絲 堂史普埃德加 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Maddox于1732年1月1日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州古倫德时，她的父亲John Maddox Sr32岁，母亲Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood30岁。她于1755年10月15日与John D. Hancock在美国弗吉尼亚州古倫德聖 加也斯 帕里什结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和8个女儿的父母。她去世于1805年在美国伊利诺伊州埃德加芭黎絲享年73岁，埋葬在美国伊利诺伊州埃德加芭黎絲 堂史普埃德加 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Maddox
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): from the Welsh personal name Madog (see Maddock ). Variant of Maddocks .

variants.name: Mattix
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

variants.name: Maddock
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): from the Welsh personal name Madawc, Madog, from the ancestor of Welsh madog ‘goodly’. As a personal name and surname it was quite common in medieval Wales, in English counties bordering Wales, and in late medieval West Cornwall.

variants.name: Maddix
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

variants.name: Maddex
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

variants.name: Maddux
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

variants.name: Mattocks
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

variants.name: Mattox
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Mount Vernon
historicEvents.description: Mount Vernon Plantation was the home of George Washington. It started off as 2,000 acres and was later expanded to 8,000 acres. The house itself started off as a six room building then got extended to twenty-one rooms.
"Patrick Henry made his ""Give me Liberty or Give me Death"" speech in Richmond Virginia."
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
Bill of Rights guarantees individual freedom.

historicEvents.title: The Bill of Rights
historicEvents.description: After the Constitution was made, some objections were raised by Anti-Federalists. So, in response ten amendments were discussed and voted on to become The Bill of Rights. These rights were made to clarify and guarantee certain freedoms of residents of the country.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Maddox was born on 1 January 1732, in Goochland, Virginia, British Colonial America, her father, John Maddox Sr, was 32 and her mother, Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood, was 30. She married John D. Hancock on 15 October 1755, in St. Jame's Parish, Goochland, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 8 daughters. She died in 1805, in Paris, Edgar, Illinois, United States, at the age of 73, and was buried in Edgar Cemetery, Paris Township, Edgar, Illinois, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Maddox est née le 1 janvier 1732, à Goochland, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, John Maddox Sr, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé John D. Hancock le 15 octobre 1755, à St. Jame's Parish, Goochland, Virginie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 8 filles. Elle est décédée en 1805, à Paris, Edgar, Illinois, États-Unis, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterrée à Edgar Cemetery, Paris Township, Edgar, Illinois, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Maddox am 1. Januar 1732 in Goochland, Virginia, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Maddox Sr 32 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood 30. Sie hat am 15. Oktober 1755 in St. Jame's Parish, Goochland, Virginia, Vereinigte Staaten John D. Hancock geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 8 Töchter. Sie starb 1805 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Paris, Edgar, Illinois, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Edgar Cemetery, Paris Township, Edgar, Illinois, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

Lei sposato John D. Hancock il 15 ottobre 1755, nel St. Jame's Parish, Goochland, Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 8 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Maddoxが1732年1月1日にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州グーチランドで生まれた時，父John Maddox Srは32歳，母Elizabeth Woodson Sherwoodは30歳であった。1755年10月15日にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州グーチランドスト. ジャム'ス パーイッシュでJohn D. Hancockと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼女は1805年にアメリカ合衆国イリノイ州エドガーパーイスで73歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国イリノイ州エドガーパーイス トウンシュイップエドガー セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Maddox(이)가 1732년 1월 1일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 구클랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Maddox Sr(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1755년 10월 15일 미국 버지니아 주 구클랜드 스트. 자'스 파리시에서 John D. Hancock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1805년 미국 일리노이 주 에드가 파리스에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 일리노이 주 에드가 파리스 타운시프 에드가 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Maddox nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1732, em Goochland, Virginia, British Colonial America, seu pai, John Maddox Sr, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com John D. Hancock em 15 de outubro de 1755, em St. Jame's Parish, Goochland, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 8 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1805, em Paris, Edgar, Ilinóis, Estados Unidos da América, com 73 anos, e foi sepultada em Edgar Cemetery, Paris Township, Edgar, Ilinóis, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Maddox родилась 1 января 1732 г. в местности гучланд, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени John Maddox Sr было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John D. Hancock 15 октября 1755 г. в местности ст. джеме'с пэриш, гучланд, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Она умерла в 1805 г. в местности пэрис, эджар, Иллинойс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 73 лет и была похоронена в местности эджар кеметири, пэрис тоуншип, эджар, Иллинойс, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Maddox nació el 1 de enero de 1732, en Goochland, Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, John Maddox Sr, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood, tenía 30 años. Se casó con John D. Hancock el 15 de octubre de 1755, en St. Jame's Parish, Goochland, Virginia, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió en 1805, en Paris, Edgar, Illinois, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultada en Edgar Cemetery, Paris Township, Edgar, Illinois, Estados Unidos.
